Image¶

In [1]:
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

import astropy.io.fits as fits
from astropy.wcs import WCS
from astropy import units as u

Download a FITS images from the The STScI Digitized Sky Survey¶

  • RA = 18h 01m 40s
  • Dec = -22d 58m 19s
  • Size: 45 x 45 arcminutes
  • Survey: POSS2 Red
  • Download a FITS images of your object
  • Upload the FITS of your object to this directory

Read in the FITS file¶

In [2]:
target_file = './dss_search'
In [3]:
fits.info(target_file)

Filename: ./dss_search
No.    Name      Ver    Type      Cards   Dimensions   Format
  0  PRIMARY       1 PrimaryHDU     161   (2673, 2679)   int16   
  1  xs.mask       1 TableHDU        25   1600R x 4C   [F6.2, F6.2, F6.2, F6.2]

Get the World Coordinate System (WCS) data from your FITS file.¶

In [4]:
target_data   = fits.getdata(target_file, 0)
target_header = fits.getheader(target_file, 0)

target_wcs = WCS(target_header)

WARNING: FITSFixedWarning: 'datfix' made the change 'Set MJD-OBS to 48506.397222 from DATE-OBS'. [astropy.wcs.wcs]

Create the following 5 plots:¶

  • Make them each a seperate plot.
  • All images should have RA and DEC coords on the labels (use WCS)
  • Put a title on each plot
    • Plot 1: A histogram of the pixel values of the target image (label the axes)
    • Plot 2: Target image
    • Plot 3: Target image where (values < MEAN set to min)
    • Plot 4: Target image where (values < (60% of MAX) set to min)
    • Plot 5: Target image where (values < (90% of MAX) set to min)

Actually change the values in the images. Don't use vmin/vmax¶

In [5]:
fig, ax = plt.subplots(
    figsize=(8, 5),
    constrained_layout=True)

fig.set_constrained_layout_pads(wspace = .15)

ax.set_title("A histogram of the pixel values of the target image")
ax.set_xlabel("Image Pixel Values")
ax.set_ylabel("Number of Pixels")
ax.hist(target_data.flatten(),bins=100);
In [6]:
fig, ax = plt.subplots(
        subplot_kw={'projection': target_wcs},
    figsize=(8, 5), constrained_layout=True)


fig.set_constrained_layout_pads(wspace = .15)

ax.set_title("Target Image")
ax.imshow(target_data, 
               cmap = plt.cm.gray,
                origin='lower')
Out[6]:
<matplotlib.image.AxesImage at 0x7dffa175ecf0>
In [7]:
target_data[target_data < target_data.mean()] = target_data.mean()


fig, ax = plt.subplots(
    subplot_kw={'projection': target_wcs},
    figsize=(8, 5), constrained_layout=True)


fig.set_constrained_layout_pads(wspace = .15)

ax.set_title("Target Image where (values < MEAN set to min)")
ax.imshow(target_data, 
               cmap = plt.cm.gray,
                origin='lower')
Out[7]:
<matplotlib.image.AxesImage at 0x7dffa15c2990>
In [8]:
target_data[target_data < 0.6 * target_data.max()] = target_data.min()


fig, ax = plt.subplots(
    subplot_kw={'projection': target_wcs},
    figsize=(8, 5), constrained_layout=True)


fig.set_constrained_layout_pads(wspace = .15)

ax.set_title("Target image where (values < (60% of MAX) set to min)")
ax.imshow(target_data, 
               cmap = plt.cm.gray,
                origin='lower')
Out[8]:
<matplotlib.image.AxesImage at 0x7dffa141a850>
In [9]:
target_data[target_data < 0.9 * target_data.max()] = target_data.min()


fig, ax = plt.subplots(
    subplot_kw={'projection': target_wcs},
    figsize=(8, 5), constrained_layout=True)


fig.set_constrained_layout_pads(wspace = .15)

ax.set_title("Target image where (values < (90% of MAX) set to min)")
ax.imshow(target_data, 
               cmap = plt.cm.gray,
                origin='lower')
Out[9]:
<matplotlib.image.AxesImage at 0x7dffa15c0cd0>

  • upload your .html file to the class Canvas page
